meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Learn how to build user onboarding and overcome 'no project ideas' paralysis. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch fast.
content.answer_block: The best way to overcome 'no project ideas' is to stop searching for a 'big idea' and start building a high-utility micro-feature. By focusing on user onboarding, you solve a critical SaaS problem while mastering state management, conditional UI flows, and database persistence—the foundational skills required for every professional production application.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the greatest enemy of the aspiring developer. You have the tools, the tutorials, and the ambition, but the dreaded 'no project ideas' paralysis keeps you stuck in tutorial hell. You are waiting for a lightning bolt of inspiration that never comes, while the real path to mastery is paved with solving mundane, high-value technical challenges.

User onboarding is the perfect antidote. Every SaaS needs it, every business struggles with it, and it forces you to build the core architecture of a real product. Stop waiting for the perfect idea and start building the bridge that guides users from curiosity to conversion. This is how you transform from a student into a builder.

steps.title: Define the User Journey Map
steps.description: Before writing code, map out the three critical steps: Welcome, Feature Highlight, and Final Call-to-Action. This ensures your onboarding isn't just a slide deck, but a functional guide.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a JSON schema for a 3-step user onboarding flow that tracks 'current_step', 'is_completed', and 'user_preferences' for a productivity app.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a React component for a multi-step onboarding modal with state management for progress tracking.'

steps.title: Build the State Machine
steps.description: Onboarding is essentially a state machine. You need to handle navigation, completion, and persistence so the user doesn't see the onboarding again after they finish.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a custom React hook named useOnboarding that manages the visibility of onboarding steps and persists state to localStorage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor the onboarding state logic to use an enum for step transitions and add a skip button functionality.'

steps.title: Implement Contextual Tooltips
steps.description: Don't just use modals; use tooltips that point to actual UI elements. This teaches you how to calculate element positioning and viewport awareness.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a reusable Tooltip component that uses absolute positioning to anchor itself to a target element ID passed via props.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add a Popper.js implementation to the Tooltip component for automatic collision detection and flipping.'

steps.title: Persist Progress to Database
steps.description: A professional app tracks user onboarding status in the database. Use an API route to update the user record once onboarding is completed.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Node.js/Express endpoint that updates a 'has_completed_onboarding' boolean field in a MongoDB user document.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Connect the frontend completion button to the backend API endpoint using an axios POST request.'

steps.title: Add Analytics Tracking
steps.description: Measure where users drop off. Adding analytics to your onboarding flow is a hallmark of a senior-level developer who thinks about business metrics.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a utility function to track onboarding step completion events using a mock analytics provider interface.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Integrate the analytics tracking function into the useOnboarding hook to log step transitions.'

pro_tips: Always build for the 'Exit'—ensure the user can skip onboarding at any point; forcing users to finish is a classic beginner mistake.
Use CSS transitions for step changes; a sudden jump in UI state feels broken and unprofessional.
Implement a 'progress bar' component—it triggers the psychological 'Zeigarnik Effect', making users more likely to complete the flow.
Never hardcode your onboarding strings; pull them from a separate JSON file to make localization (i18n) easier later.
Test your onboarding flow on mobile first; if it breaks on a small screen, it will fail in production.
content.next_steps: Now that you have a functional onboarding flow, integrate it into a real project dashboard. Use this as the 'hero' feature in your portfolio to show potential employers you understand both UI/UX and backend persistence.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting the 'no project ideas' myth paralyze your potential. You have the guide, the tools, and the technical roadmap to build something that actually matters. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
